answer: ValidationError indicates failures in payload/DTO structural validation (e.g., required fields missing) and should be used in the application or infrastructure layers when incoming request data fails basic structural validation — it maps to HTTP 400 Bad Request. InvalidDataError indicates domain-specific business rule validation failures and should be used in domain entities, value objects, and builders when business invariants or domain validations fail — it maps to HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity.
